rulings.Case Title: JOHNNIE L. BROWN VS. DENNIS J. HERRERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Defts Disclosure Or Discovery, Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 2, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOHNNIE BROWN Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Defts Disclosure Or Discovery, Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/EG

rulings.Case Title: RONALD SCOTT VS. BAE SYSTEMS, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Sever Pltf'S Claims For Trial And Index Of Non-California Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANTs BAE SYSTEMS, INC., BAE SYSTEMS, SHIP REPAIR INC., BAE SYSTEMS, SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC., HUGH VANDERSPEK, JIM VAUGHT's Motion To Sever Plaintiff's Claims For Trial. The motion to sever is denied. The cases were consolidated for trial purposes only because complete consolidation would have been improper under Sanchezv. Sup. Crt, (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1391, 1396. Defendants have the right to bring motions for summary judgment/adjudication directed at each individual plaintiff's claims. The parties should continue to coordinate discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Allan Pont M.D And Sutter West Bay Hospital'S (Dba California Pacific) Demurrer To 5th Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2013, Line 11, Defendants Allan Pont M.D And Sutter West Bay Hospital'S (Dba California Pacific) Demurrer To 5th Amended Complaint. Defendant Paraguya's demurrer is sustained, without leave to amend. Mr. Butler's claim of fraud is barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP § 340.5.) CCP § 340.5 applies to a cause of action for deceit based on the physician's false representations or fraudulent concealment of the nature and extent of the injury. (Weinstock v. Eissler (2002) 224 Cal.App.2d 212, 227.) "Notwithstanding a defendant's continuing efforts to conceal, if plaintiff discovers the claim independently, the limitations period commences." (Sanchez v. South Hoover Hospital (1976) 18 Cal.3d 93, 99.) Further, Plaintiff has alleged that the last overt act of conspiracy that was May 2, 2010 when the California Department of Public Health sending a letter to Mr. Butler. The 1 year Statute of Limitations would still have expired May 1, 2011, before Plaintiff filed the original complaint Jude 29, 2011. Also, the Court cannot construe the 90 day letter Plaintiff allegedly sent to Defendants to toll the statute of limitations by 90 days per CCP § 364 because the letter is undated, and Defendants did not have actual notice of the letter. (See Hanooka v. Pivko (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 1553, 1560.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT LAUREEN PARAGUYA'S DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Southwest Bay Hospital' s demurrer is sustained, without leave to amend. Mr. Butler's claim of fraud is barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP § 340.5.) CCP § 340.5 applies to a cause of action for deceit based on the physician's false representations or fraudulent concealment of the nature and extent of the injury. (Weinstock v. Eissler (2002) 224 Cal.App.2d 212, 227.) "Notwithstanding a defendant's continuing efforts to conceal, if plaintiff discovers the claim independently, the limitations period commences." (Sanchez v. South Hoover Hospital (1976) 18 Cal.3d 93, 99.) Further, Plaintiff has alleged that the last overt act of conspiracy that was May 2, 2010 when the California Department of Public Health sending a letter to Mr. Butler. The 1 year Statute of Limitations would still have expired May 1, 2011, before Plaintiff filed the original complaint Jude 29, 2011. Also, the Court cannot construe the 90 day letter Plaintiff allegedly sent to Defendants to toll the statute of limitations by 90 days per CCP § 364 because the letter is undated, and Defendants did not have actual notice of the letter. (See Hanooka v. Pivko (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 1553, 1560.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RUBECON GENERAL CONTRACTING INC VS. APARTMENT INVESTMENT AND MANAGEMENT COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2013, Line 16, CROSS DEFENDANT FORTNEY & WEYGANDT, INC.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. This motion is off calendar because of insufficient service per CCP § 1005, which requires at least 16 court days for notice of hearing, and an additional 5 calendar days for service by mail per CCP § 1005(b). This motion should re-noticed for hearing in Department 501. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CONOR O'BRIEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Francois Ekoule And Always The Best Limousine Service (Abls) Motion For Protective Order To Limit Number Of Disclosed Expert Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 2, 2013, Line 7, Defendants Francois Ekoule And Always The Best Limousine Service (Abls) Motion For Protective Order To Limit Number Of Disclosed Expert Witnesses Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY SCHEUMANN VS. MICHAEL A. ALFARO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Application For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2013, Line 26, PLAINTIFF GEOFFREY SCHEUMANN's Application For Preliminary Injunction. The Court grants the motion as to the Alfaros. Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the trespass and nuisance causes of action. Injunctive relief is warranted to prevent the ongoing harm. The Court denies the motion as to the tenants. There is no showing that they were ever served or have notice of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
